title: -Purchase Order Amendment: Braun Northwest Inc. for Two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star Engine Remount of Ambulance Bodies to New Chassis

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend the purchase order with
Braun Northwest, Inc. to purchase two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star Ambulance
chassis by increasing the purchase order amount by $75,000 for a total not-to-exceed
amount of $505,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The purchase of two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star ambulance chassis will not exceed
$505,000.00. The increase in the not-to-exceed amount is needed as the previous
authorization did not include sales tax. The funding necessary for this purchase is
available in the approved FY 2025 Budget, Equipment Replacement Fund 671.
Total
Current Purchase Order Amount $430,000
Proposed Amendment Increase $75,000
Total Not-To-Exceed Amount $505,000
sections.current_situation:
This order will replace the chassis of two ambulances (Equipment ID 19001 and
19003) that are nearing the end of their useful service life. Both vehicles were put in
service in 2020 and are planned for replacement in July 2026. The average time to
remount and upfit an ambulance is 12 or more months which is why the purchase is
being requested at this time. One ambulance chassis was ordered with previous
Council authorization on December 10, 2024, through Resolution No. 71,623-N.S.
The existing ambulance bodies are customized for optimal Fire Department use and
are in good condition. These bodies will be remounted on new chassis to extend the

For Two (2) 2025 North Star Engine Remounts April 29, 2025
useful life of the vehicles. The practice of remounting ambulance bodies on new
chassis rather than purchasing new ambulances is an effective cost saving measure
as it will save over $100,000. This amendment is necessary as additional funding is
needed to cover the sales tax for these purchases.
Both of the existing chassis will be repurposed and equipped with emergency response
capabilities so that they can be used in the event of a Fire Department emergency
event.
These vehicles are used 24/7 by the City’s Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Division
in the course of their job duties.
This purchase agreement will support the City’s Strategic Plan Goal of creating a
resilient, safe, connected, and prepared City.
sections.background:
If a purchase request exceeds $100,000.00, the Department of Finance, General
Services Division may solicit bids or leverage an existing contract that was
competitively bid by another entity to purchase similar goods or services.
The City has been a no-cost member of the Houston-Galveston Area Council
(HGACBuy), a Cooperative Purchasing Program that has offered inter-local
purchasing since 2008. HGACBuy provides procurement services that make the
government procurement process more efficient by establishing competitively priced
contracts for goods and services made available to local governments nationwide.
All products and services offered through HGACBuy have been subjected to a public
competitive bid process. On June 9, 2023, HGACBuy released a Request for Proposal
No. AM 10-23 for Ambulances, EMS and Other Special Service Vehicles. The
solicitation was nationally advertised for forty-eight days (48). On August 10, 2023,
twenty-three (23) proposals were submitted and reviewed. On October 1, 2023,
Houston-Galveston Area Council selected Braun Northwest, Inc. as the best
responsive and responsible proposers to meet the specifications, thusly awarding
Contract No. AM10-23.
HGACBuy charges an order processing charge for each sale successfully completed
through their contract. For this purchase, a flat fee of $4,000.00 will be paid directly
to HGACBuy by Braun Northwest, Inc.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
For fleet purchases, Public Works staff researches the feasibility and availability of fully
electric vehicles and hybrid-electric powertrains to support the City’s transition off fossil
fuels. Staff research found that there are no viable electric or hybrid electric ambulance
chassis for consideration. The Fire Department would like to transition its fleet of front-
line EMS vehicles to electric vehicles. This is likely to not be financially feasible until all

sections.rationale:
The vehicles are in service now and the chassis are past their useful life. These vehicle
bodies are in good condition and can be remounted on new chassis. A purchase order
is needed to purchase new chassis and transfer the current ambulance bodies.
Ambulances in good working condition are essential to respond, provide treatment and
transport members of our community that experience medical emergencies. The
existing chassis will be repurposed as utility units, equipped with emergency response
capabilities and considered new to fleet emergency service vehicles.
sections.alternatives:
The purchase of new ambulances is cost prohibitive. The alternative would be full vehicle
replacement and there is insufficient funding in the Equipment Replacement Fund 671 to
accommodate that option.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Don Colbert, Sr. Equipment Maintenance Supervisor, (510) 981-6467
David Sprague, Fire Chief (510) 981-3473
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PURCHASE ORDER AMENDMENT WITH BRAUN NORTHWEST, INC FOR TWO (2)
2025 FORD F-450 NORTH STAR ENGINE REMOUNTS OF AMBULANCE BODIES
TO NEW CHASSIS
WHEREAS, on December 10, 2024, by Resolution No. 71,623-N.S. the City Council
authorized a Purchase Order with Braun Northwest Inc. in an amount not-to-exceed
$430,000 for Two (2) 2025 Ford F-450 North Star Engine remounts of ambulance bodies
to new chassis; and
WHEREAS, this purchase will replace engine chassis that are nearing the end of their
useful service life as front line, initial response vehicles. Ambulances in good repair are
needed by the City of Berkeley Fire Department to provide ambulatory services; and
WHEREAS, the City has been a no-cost member of the Houston-Galveston Area
Council (HGACBuy), a Cooperative Purchasing Program that has offered inter-local
purchasing since 2008: and
WHEREAS, on June 9, 2023, HGACBuy released a Request for Proposal No. AM 10-23
for Ambulances, EMS and Other Special Service Vehicles. The solicitation was nationally
advertised for forty-eight days (48). On August 10, 2023, twenty-three (23) proposals
were submitted and reviewed. On October 1, 2023, HGACBuy selected Braun
Northwest, Inc. as the best responsive and responsible proposers to meet the
specifications, thusly awarding Contract No. AM10-23; and
WHEREAS, the current purchase order amount not-to-exceed is insufficient to pay the
sales tax which was not included in the previous purchase order cost and the City has
need of Braun Northwest Inc. chassis remount of ambulance bodies; and
WHEREAS, additional funding in the amount of $75,000.00 will be available in the FY
2025 baseline budget in the Equipment Replacement Fund (671).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or his designee is hereby authorized to amend the purchase order with
Braun Northwest Inc. for two (2) ambulance engine remounts to new chassis in the
amount
